text.title: Migration Workflow and Agent Pitfalls
text.summary: Steps for migrating a legacy token set to W3C draft format, CI validation pattern, and common agent mistakes.

rule.statement: After migrating, run a rendered-output snapshot diff (CSS, Swift, XML) to verify semantic preservation before shipping.
rule.rationale: A format migration that changes rendered values is a visual regression; the snapshot diff catches it before users do.

list.item: Audit current token structure: identify primitive, semantic, and component layers.
Align naming to `$type`/`$value`/`$ref` fields; add `$description` to every semantic token.
Validate alias graph: every `$ref` must resolve within the file without circular references.
Run a CI validator against the pinned draft snapshot date on every PR.
Pair migration with a rendered-output snapshot test before promoting to production.

section.title: CI Validation

rule.statement: Gate CI on W3C draft compliance using a schema validator; add a "format date" check to flag when the upstream spec has moved.
rule.rationale: Without CI gating, non-compliant tokens accumulate; catching them at PR time costs far less than post-migration cleanup.

section.reference: Shell script that validates a token file against the pinned W3C draft JSON Schema.

antipattern.description: Agent confuses W3C draft format with Tokens Studio JSON dialect.
antipattern.reason: Both use `$type` but differ on composite type sub-schemas and alias syntax; explicitly specify the target format and version in every prompt.

antipattern.description: Generated `$type` values use older names (e.g., `type` instead of `$type`).
antipattern.reason: The `$`-prefix is required by the draft; validators reject the unprefixed form. Enforce the `$`-prefixed form in a post-generation lint step.

antipattern.description: Composite types (shadow, gradient, typography) generated with incorrect sub-schemas.
antipattern.reason: Composite type sub-schemas have evolved across draft versions; lint each composite against its specific sub-schema after generation.

antipattern.description: Agent assumes Style Dictionary v4 syntax without checking the project version.
antipattern.reason: v3 and v4 have incompatible transform APIs; pin the version explicitly in every agent prompt to avoid silent no-output bugs.
